title: eBPF not supported: OCI runtime error
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**
/kind bug

**Description**
Hi,
I would like to use the `--device` option, but I get the following error:
```
$  sudo podman run -it --rm --device=/dev/dri/card0:/dev/dri/card0 archlinux
Error: eBPF not supported: OCI runtime error
```
Instead if I do not use the `--device` option it works as expected.
Is there something wrong with my kernel configuration?

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1. `sudo podman run -it --rm --device=/dev/dri/card0:/dev/dri/card0 archlinux`
2.
3.

**Describe the results you received:**
`Error: eBPF not supported: OCI runtime error`

**Describe the results you expected:**
Container running

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**
```
Version:            1.8.2
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.14.1
Built:              Sun Apr 12 10:09:43 2020
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**
```
debug:
  compiler: gc
  git commit: ""
  go version: go1.14.1
  podman version: 1.8.2
host:
  BuildahVersion: 1.14.3
  CgroupVersion: v2
  Conmon:
    package: Unknown
    path: /usr/libexec/podman/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 2.0.11, commit: ff9d97a08d7a4b58267ac03719786e4e7258cecf'
  Distribution:
    distribution: gentoo
    version: unknown
  MemFree: 9677873152
  MemTotal: 16494366720
  OCIRuntime:    name: crun
    package: Unknown
    path: /usr/bin/crun
    version: |-
      crun version 0.10.6      spec: 1.0.0
      +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +YAJL
  SwapFree: 0
  SwapTotal: 0
  arch: amd64
  cpus: 8
  eventlogger: journald
  hostname: janis
  kernel: 5.6.3
  os: linux
  rootless: false
  uptime: 1h 55m 52.78s (Approximately 0.04 days)
registries:
  search:
  - docker.io
  - quay.io
  - registry.fedoraproject.org
store:
  ConfigFile: /etc/containers/storage.conf
  ContainerStore:
    number: 0
  GraphDriverName: overlay
  GraphOptions:
    overlay.mount_program:
      Executable: /usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs
      Package: Unknown
      Version: |-
        fusermount3 version: 3.9.1
        fuse-overlayfs: version 0.7.8
        FUSE library version 3.9.1
        using FUSE kernel interface version 7.31
  GraphRoot: /home/vasco/.local/share/containers/storage
  GraphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: xfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "false"
    Supports d_type: "true"
    Using metacopy: "false"
  ImageStore:
    number: 8
  RunRoot: /run/user/1000
  VolumePath: /home/vasco/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**
```
[ebuild   R    ] app-emulation/libpod-1.8.2::gentoo  USE="rootless -apparmor -btrfs (-selinux)" 0 KiB
```

comments.body:
the devices controller on cgroup v2 uses eBPF.

Secure Boot might be blocking eBPF (`man 7 kernel_lockdown`), could you please verify if that is the case by disabling it?

Yes secure boot is disabled.

Thanks

On Mon, 13 Apr 2020, 08:14 Giuseppe Scrivano, <notifications@github.com>
wrote:

> the devices controller on cgroup v2 uses eBPF.
>
> Secure Boot might be blocking eBPF (man 7 kernel_lockdown), could you
> please verify if that is the case by disabling it?
>
> —
> You are receiving this because you authored the thread.
> Reply to this email directly, view it on GitHub
> <https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/5795#issuecomment-612783540>,
> or unsubscribe
> <https://github.com/notifications/unsubscribe-auth/AAM5GSVGCTNJPZGZRNRDMX3RMK3TVANCNFSM4MGMYGGQ>
> .
>
does eBPF generally work?  Are you able to use `execsnoop`?

Sorry, how can I test eBPF?
I have:
```
$ execsnoop
command not found: execsnoop
```

Thanks

I solved by enabling the flag `bpf` for `app-emulation/crun`.

Thanks
thanks for letting me know.  Is it something that should be fixed in the gentoo package?

Maybe enable by default the bpf flag for crun?

On Mon, 13 Apr 2020, 11:12 Giuseppe Scrivano, <notifications@github.com>
wrote:

> thanks for letting me know. Is it something that should be fixed in the
> gentoo package?
>
> —
> You are receiving this because you modified the open/close state.
> Reply to this email directly, view it on GitHub
> <https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/5795#issuecomment-612840208>,
> or unsubscribe
> <https://github.com/notifications/unsubscribe-auth/AAM5GSX3JZSDIYYNCVO3UK3RMLQSPANCNFSM4MGMYGGQ>
> .
>

> Maybe enable by default the bpf flag for crun?

it is, but dependencies must be installed.
